title: The Nyquist mode of a constant two-point signal is zero

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a constant two-point signal (u,u), the Nyquist Fourier mode equals zero.
assumptions: the two-point Nyquist mode is u0 - u1

declarations.name: dft2Nyquist
declarations.statement: def dft2Nyquist (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 - u1

declarations.name: dft2Nyquist_const
declarations.statement: theorem dft2Nyquist_const (u : Real) : dft2Nyquist u u = 0

tasks.input: Translate the vanishing Nyquist mode of a constant two-point signal into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem dft2Nyquist_const (u : Real) : dft2Nyquist u u = 0

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding the Nyquist mode and simplifying.
